title: Globalisation, poverty and inequalities
summary: 9699 — global poverty, inequality between and within nations, and development debates.
learningObjectives: Measure global poverty and inequality indicators.
Explain how globalisation affects rich and poor nations.
Evaluate aid, trade, and debt relief policies.

simpleExplanation.title: Winners and losers globally
simpleExplanation.summary: Globalisation has lifted some regions out of poverty while deepening inequality elsewhere — development theories disagree on why.
steps: Aid, fair trade, and structural adjustment.

sections.content: **Poverty and inequality** (8.1) uses **World Bank/UN data**. Contrast **modernisation vs dependency**.
